rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Shall we go to the market tomorrow morning ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Let's rise early and leave after the bath.

rows.speaker: C
rows.english: Dad, let's leave after breakfast,

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: puuje maadi hoogbeeku  puuje maadde hoogbaardu.
rows.english: We should go only after worshipping. We shouldn't leave without worshipping.

rows.speaker: C
rows.english: Let's go after worshipping and having breakfast.

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Let's dine out and return home.

rows.speaker: C
rows.roman: amma, kaamat hootlige hoogi uuta maadoona.
rows.english: Mummy, let's go to Hotel 'Kamat' and dine there.

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: naayiina maneelee bittu, pakkada maneyavarge heeli hoogoona.
rows.english: Let's leave the dog at home, request the nighbours to mind the house and go.

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: oohoo ! banni banni, uurige yavaag bandri ?
rows.english: Oh ! please come in ; when did you arrive in town ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: We came yesterday.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Please dine with us.

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Excuse us, we have had lunch.

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: naale uuta maadde barbeeku. iiga svalpa kaafi kudiiri. Kaafi kudiide hoogbaardu.
rows.english: Please come without lunch tomorrow. Have a sip of coffee now ; you should not leave without a cup of coffee.

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: iig beeda, kaafi kandre aagalla. naale uuta maadde bartiivi. illee uuta maadtiivi. iig hoogbeeku.
rows.english: Not now, please, Can't stand coffee. We will come on an empty stomach tomorrow and eat here. We have to leave now.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Bye, bye !

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Good bye !
to wake up
window
worship
door
one by the side
shop
